title: Delissea lauliiana
taxonomy: Delissea lauliiana is a species of flowering plant in the heath family, Ericaceae. It is native to the Hawaiian Islands. The plant belongs to the genus Delissea, which consists of several species of flowering plants. Delissea lauliiana is typically classified as a shrub or small tree. It is considered an endemic species, meaning it is only found in its native range.
uses: Delissea lauliiana, also known as the Mauna Kea hedyosmum, has been used by local communities in Hawaii for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
